title: Rootful Podman machine cannot bind to a host IP
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

A container attempting to bind to a host IP results in an error in a rootful Podman machine.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init --rootful --now`

2. `sudo ifconfig lo0 alias 192.168.64.1` (creates an alias to the loopback device) 

3. `podman run -it -p 192.168.64.1:8888:8888 alpine`

**Describe the results you received:**

The container does not start and the following error is displayed:

```
Error: error preparing container e9accb477e9dfffd8790b5adec96fb031e7b82beeb60403ba647a2d1f75403be for attach: cannot listen on the TCP port: listen tcp4 192.168.64.1:8888: bind: cannot assign requested address
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A running container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This happens on a fresh machine, so restarting/recreating doesn't solve it.

Tried with a variety of IP addresses without success.

The following two scenarios do work fine, however:

- `podman run -it -p 8888:8888 alpine` (no host IP seems ok)

- `podman run -it -p 127.0.0.1:8888:8888 alpine` (binding to 127.0.0.1 seems ok)

- `podman -c podman-machine-default run -it -p 192.168.64.1:8888:8888 alpine` (rootless Podman machine seems ok)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

comments.body: Any chance you can provide a `podman inspect` of a container which shows this symptom? Want to make sure we're just binding to that one IP.
@mheon The container never does start due to the error. I've updated the OP to mention this now.
I think I know the problem. We try to bind the ports as root in libpod but I don't call the magic machine port conversion function there.

Dang, that was fast! 🎉 

I'd be happy to test it out if you could point me to some steps on how I'd go about it.

For this patch you only have to change the podman binary in the VM.
Since the VM is fedora35 you can copy the binary from the CI build https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4863906593112064, just click on binary under the Artifacts section. You need the "podman" binary there.
In your VM activate rpm-ostree usroverlay with `podman machine ssh sudo rpm-ostree usroverlay`, then you should be able to copy the new binary to `/usr/bin/podman` in the VM. This will make the machine use the new binary. Note that this change is only active until the next reboot of the VM.
Oh, the binary you've linked to is x86 but since I'm on an M1 MacBook I'd need an arm64 version. I've looked around that CI task but couldn't find such a version.
Ah yeah, we do have arm systems in CI unfortunately. You would need to compile from source then.
Verified fixed with a build from source, thanks!
Would I be right in assuming this fix will make it into v4.0.3? =)
